name: layering-violation
description: Dashboard UI component imports the database layer directly, and a feature module reaches into another feature's internals.

pr.title: Show live query count on Dashboard
pr.author: sam
pr.description: Display the number of active queries directly on the Dashboard. Pulls the count from the database and wires it into the analytics feature for display.

agentResponse.summary: The Dashboard component imports the database layer directly, bypassing the service layer, and also reaches into the analytics feature's internal implementation rather than its public API.

findings.title: UI component imports the database layer directly
findings.detail: Dashboard.tsx imports getActiveQueryCount from src/db/queries.js. UI components should call service or hook abstractions, not the database layer directly.

findings.title: Tight coupling: feature module reaches into another feature internals
findings.detail: src/features/a/index.ts re-exports from analytics/internal/AnalyticsBar.js. Cross-feature imports should go through the target feature's public index, not its internal paths.

boundaryViolations: UI component imports DB layer directly (Dashboard.tsx → src/db/queries.js)
couplingConcerns: Feature A reaches into analytics feature internals (features/a → analytics/internal/AnalyticsBar)
